data.title: Plantify Foods announces liquidation of subsidiary
data.description:
• Plantify Foods (TSXV:PTFY:CA) said that its wholly owned subsidiary, Peas of Bean (POB) has filed a request with the district court of Nof Hagalil-Nazareth, Israel to initiate proceedings for the purpose of liquidation.
  • Due to the continuous drone attacks, missile strikes and shootings in the region, the area has been almost completely evacuated of civilians. 
  • Plantify Foods (PTFY:CA) has no other business or material assets and intends to commence the evaluation of potential business opportunities to replace POB.

data.title: Financial Institutions prices $95M stock offering
data.description:
• Financial Institutions (NASDAQ:FISI) priced its previously announced underwritten public offering of 4M shares at a public offering price of $25.00 per share.
  • The expected proceeds are ~$95M.
  • Underwriters are granted a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 600K shares.
  • Net proceeds to be used for general corporate purposes which may include a balance sheet restructuring through the repositioning of a substantial portion of our available-for-sale debt securities portfolio to support continued growth as well as the repayment of indebtedness.
  • The stock price dropped 2% on Thursday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: Ormat Technologies prices $281.9M secondary offering on behalf of shareholder ORIX
data.description:
• Ormat Technologies (NYSE:ORA) announces pricing of a secondary offering of 3.7M shares of common stock on behalf of ORIX at $76.20 per share, for expected proceeds of $281.9M.
  • Underwriters' overall allotment is 555,000 shares of Ormat’s common stock from ORIX Corporation.
  • The offering is expected to close on December 13, 2024

  • ORA shares were trading -5.1% after-hours on Wednesday.

data.title: ServiceTitan prices IPO at $71 per share
data.description:
• Software platform that powers trades businesses—ServiceTitan (TTAN) priced its initial public offering of 8.8M shares at a public offering price of $71.00 per share.
  • Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1.32M shares.
  • The shares are expected to begin trading on the Nasdaq Global Select Market on December 12, 2024, under the ticker symbol “TTAN.”
  • The offering is expected to close on December 13, 2024.

data.title: AmpliTech Group jumps 40%, prices $2.2M direct stock offering
data.description:
•  AmpliTech Group (NASDAQ:AMPG) entered into one or more securities purchase agreements with certain institutional investors to purchase ~1.35M shares at $1.60 per share, in a registered direct offering.

  • The gross proceeds are estimated to be ~$2.2M.

  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 13, 2024.

  • The stock price jumped ~40% on Wednesday during after market hours of trading.

data.title: NewAmsterdam Pharma prices $416M stock offering
data.description:
• NewAmsterdam Pharma Company (NASDAQ:NAMS) priced its previously announced underwritten public offering of ~12.12M at a public offering price of $24.50 per share and pre-funded warrants to certain investors.
  • The proceeds are expected to be ~$416.5M.
  • Underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 2.55M shares.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about December 13, 2024.

data.title: 3D Systems to divest Geomagic Software to Hexagon for $123M
data.description:
• 3D Systems (NYSE:DDD) has announced the sale of its Geomagic® software portfolio to Hexagon's Manufacturing Intelligence Division for $123 million.

  • Upon closing the divestiture, 3D Systems will focus on its core software platforms: 3D Sprint®, 3DXpert®, and Oqton Industrial Manufacturing OS by leveraging AI and machine learning to deliver improved workflows, higher productivity, reduced costs and improved component quality in production environments

  • This will enhance customer and shareholder value by focusing on R&D efforts, enabling customers to innovate and address diverse applications.

  • The transaction is expected to close in the first half of 2025.

data.title: Nvidia increases headcount in China to boost AI-driven car technology - report
data.description:
Chip maker Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) has significantly increased its headcount in China this year, with a focus on enhancing its research capabilities for autonomous technology in self-driven cars, Bloomberg reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

The company is expected to close 2024 with ~4,000 employees in China, up from about 3,000 at the start of the year. This expansion includes an addition of around 200 new staff members in Beijing specifically dedicated to developing self-driving technologies, the report explained.

The California-based chipmaker also expanded its networking software development and after-sales service teams in the country.

The move comes amidst a push for autonomous driving technology in China, with companies like Baidu (BIDU) testing robotaxi services.

Despite ongoing U.S.-China trade tensions, Nvidia (NVDA) reported $5.4 billion in the September quarter. The country remains a crucial market and a research hub for the U.S. tech giant, which has been actively working on autonomous driving and AI software.

The company's operations remain under scrutiny from Chinese regulators relating to its previous acquisition of Mellanox, which could potentially lead to fines or other measures.

data.title: TAT Technologies inks $17M MRO contract with major North American cargo carrier
data.description:
• TAT Technologies (NASDAQ:TATT) announced that it has signed a five-year agreement with a major North American cargo carrier, for an estimated contract value of approximately $17 million.
  • As per the contract terms, TAT will provide repair and overhaul services under an MRO agreement for auxiliary power units ((APUs)) on the Carriers' Boeing 757 and 767 aircraft.

data.title: Amaroq identifies high-grade gold at Eagle’s Nest exploration project
data.description:
• Amaroq Minerals (TSXV:AMRQ:CA) on Thursday reported the results of its recent sampling campaign at the Eagle’s Nest target within the Anoritooq licence area, part of the Nanortalik Gold Belt, located 31km from the Nalunaq Gold Mine.
  • The findings, which confirm the presence of two distinct gold quartz veins, align with Amaroq’s strategic focus on developing satellite targets to support operations at the newly commissioned Nalunaq processing facility.
  • Sampling identified gold grades up to 54.5 g/t Au in quartz veins.
  • Eagle’s Nest is one of nine satellite targets in proximity to Nalunaq. These include the previously drilled Vagar Ridge, with surface grades of up to 2,533g/t Au, alongside other reconnaissance targets, each demonstrating potential for significant orogenic gold deposits.
  • Source: Press Release
